exercises.title: Read Kernel and Host Info
exercises.description: Inspect uname and procfs to identify your kernel version and current process info.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can identify the kernel version and key fields for the current process.

exercises.title: Inspect Control Groups
exercises.description: Find the cgroup membership of your current shell and interpret the paths.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can see which cgroups your process belongs to.

exercises.title: Test File Permissions
exercises.description: Create a file, change its mode, and verify access as different users or groups.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can explain how owner, group, and other permissions change access.

exercises.title: Namespace Sandbox
exercises.description: Use unshare to create a new namespace and inspect how the process view changes.
exercises.expected_outcome: You get a shell with isolated user, mount, or network context.

exercises.title: Service Unit
exercises.description: Write and start a simple systemd service or timer and verify its logs and restart behavior.
exercises.expected_outcome: The service starts under systemd supervision and its logs are visible with journalctl.

exercises.title: Capability Demo
exercises.description: Grant a binary a single capability and compare its behavior with and without that capability.
exercises.expected_outcome: The binary can perform the privileged action without full root access.
